report_name: Virginia Man with Cybercrime History Convicted for Deleting 96 Government Databases

threat_actors.name: Akhter
threat_actors.description: A former database administrator with a prior history of federal cybercrime convictions, found guilty of deliberately deleting 96 U.S. government databases by abusing his privileged access.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor, a former database administrator, used his own privileged account or a compromised one to access and delete government databases.

attack_patterns.name: Data Destruction
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor intentionally deleted 96 government databases, likely using commands such as `DROP DATABASE` or by deleting the underlying files.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor, as a knowledgeable administrator, may have also deleted or corrupted backups to prevent recovery from the data destruction.

identities.name: U.S. Government
identities.description: A U.S. government agency that was the victim of a malicious insider attack, resulting in the deletion of 96 of its databases.

indicators.name: Destructive Database Commands Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Destructive Database Commands Execution
id: 2b8a1c3d-f4e5-4b6c-8d9e-0f1a2b3c4d5e
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of highly destructive database commands such as 'DROP DATABASE' or 'TRUNCATE TABLE', which could indicate a malicious insider or compromised account activity.
references:
  - https://recordedfuture.news/briefs/virginia-man-found-guilty-of-deleting-96-government-databases
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-09
tags:
  - attack.t1485
  - attack.impact
logsource:
  category: database_query
detection:
  keywords:
    - 'DROP DATABASE'
    - 'TRUNCATE TABLE'
    - 'DROP TABLE'
  condition: keywords
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate, albeit rare, database maintenance by authorized administrators.
level: high
indicators.description: Detects the execution of highly destructive database commands such as 'DROP DATABASE' or 'TRUNCATE TABLE', which could indicate a malicious insider or compromised account activity.

indicators.name: Backup Service Tampering
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Backup Service Tampering
id: 9e8f7d6c-5b4a-4c3d-2b1a-0f9e8d7c6b5a
status: experimental
description: Detects attempts to stop or disable common backup services, which could be a precursor to a ransomware attack or data destruction event.
references:
  - https://recordedfuture.news/briefs/virginia-man-found-guilty-of-deleting-96-government-databases
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-09
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_net:
    Image|endswith: '\net.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'stop'
  selection_sc:
    Image|endswith: '\sc.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'stop'
  selection_services:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'Veeam'
      - 'BackupExec'
      - 'wbengine'
      - 'vss'
      - 'SQLWriter'
  condition: (selection_net or selection_sc) and selection_services
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative action to restart or troubleshoot backup services.
level: medium
indicators.description: Detects attempts to stop or disable common backup services, which could be a precursor to a ransomware attack or data destruction event.
